title: Loss Prevention Camera System Uptime
spl:
index=retail sourcetype="camera:status"
| eval frame_age=now()-last_frame_epoch
| eval up=if(lower(stream_state)="up" AND frame_age < 60 AND bitrate_kbps > 100, 1, 0)
| stats avg(up) as up_frac by store_id, camera_id
| eval uptime_pct=round(up_frac*100,2)
| where uptime_pct < 99
| table store_id, camera_id, uptime_pct
description: Operational visibility into VMS/NVR stream health supports store safety and incident review workflows without duplicating fraud analytics covered elsewhere.
value: Service and risk leaders use signals from Loss Prevention Camera System Uptime to move crews, budget, and customer or regulator communications while the situation is still controllable, so the organization steers from live data rather than a post-incident report.
implementation: Poll or stream VMS health every minute; normalize `stream_state` vocabulary. Exclude planned maintenance windows via lookup. Escalate to LP and facilities when entire aisles show degraded uptime. **Domain context:** Video retention policies (e.g. 30–90 days) are separate from stream health—health proves capability to capture, not retention compliance. **Splunk:** Bitrate thresholds vary by resolution/codec—tune `bitrate_kbps` per camera model.
visualization: Heatmap (camera × hour uptime), Table (worst cameras), Single value (stores below target).
knownFalsePositives: Camera or stream issues during NVR storage rotation, LPR privacy masking, or network maintenance loss prevention and store security pre-approved.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: VMS health feed via HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=retail` `sourcetype="camera:status"` (store_id, camera_id, stream_state, bitrate_kbps, last_frame_epoch).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Poll or stream VMS health every minute; normalize `stream_state` vocabulary. Exclude planned maintenance windows via lookup. Escalate to LP and facilities when entire aisles show degraded uptime. **Domain context:** Video retention policies (e.g. 30–90 days) are separate from stream health—health proves capability to capture, not retention compliance. **Splunk:** Bitrate thresholds vary by resolution/codec—tune `bitrate_kbps` per camera model.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=retail sourcetype="camera:status"
| eval frame_age=now()-last_frame_epoch
| eval up=if(lower(stream_state)="up" AND frame_age < 60 AND bitrate_kbps > 100, 1, 0)
| stats avg(up) as up_frac by store_id, camera_id
| eval uptime_pct=round(up_frac*100,2)
| where uptime_pct < 99
| table store_id, camera_id, uptime_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Loss Prevention Camera System Uptime** — Operational visibility into VMS/NVR stream health supports store safety and incident review workflows without duplicating fraud analytics covered elsewhere.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=retail` `sourcetype="camera:status"` (store_id, camera_id, stream_state, bitrate_kbps, last_frame_epoch). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): VMS health feed via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: retail; **sourcetype**: camera:status. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=retail, sourcetype="camera:status". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **frame_age** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **up** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by store_id, camera_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uptime_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where uptime_pct < 99` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Loss Prevention Camera System Uptime**): table store_id, camera_id, uptime_pct


Step 3 — Validate
Reconcile amounts and counts with the POS journal or store close report for the same register and shift. Validate that returns, voids, and training transactions are tagged so they can be filtered if needed.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heatmap (camera × hour uptime), Table (worst cameras), Single value (stores below target).
grandmaExplanation: We watch loss-prevention camera uptime. We help you catch blind spots before incidents have no video.
